channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: As Attacks Shake Markets, Trump Seeks to Reassure Americans
item.description: President Trump said he would do whatever was necessary to lower oil prices, and his Treasury secretary said the government might even take the paradoxical step of lifting sanctions on some Iranian oil.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: The Iran War’s Economic Threat to Europe and Asia
item.description: Inflation and its consequences for growth are a growing concern for countries where memories of the 2022 energy crisis are fresh.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson and River Akira Davis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: A fuel station in Dhaka, Bangladesh, last week.

item.title: How Immigration Became an Issue in Argentina
item.description: Argentina has often stood out for its openness to immigration. Under President Javier Milei, it has started to crack down.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola
item.media:credit: Sarah Pabst for The New York Times
item.media:description: Pedestrians and clients walk through an Andean market in the Liniers section of Buenos Aires in February. The neighborhood is an immigrant hub.

item.title: The Soldier Who Came Back From the Dead
item.description: Told that Nazar Daletskyi had died, his Ukrainian family buried what they thought were his remains. He turned up three years later in a prisoner-of-war exchange.
item.dc:creator: Maria Varenikova, Evelina Riabenko and Oksana Parafeniuk
item.media:credit: Oksana Parafeniuk for The New York Times
item.media:description: Nazar Daletskyi at his own grave in the village of Velykyi Doroshiv in western Ukraine on Sunday.

item.title: Trump and Iran Dominate the Agenda as Europe’s Leaders Meet. Here Are 4 Things to Know.
item.description: A gathering in Brussels was meant to concentrate on jump-starting the E.U. economy. Instead, the fallout of the war in the Middle East took front and center.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ursula von der Leyen, president of the European Commission, in Brussels on Thursday. During their talks, E.U. leaders focused in part on escalating fuel prices.

item.title: A Crucial Loan for Ukraine Becomes Election Leverage for Orban
item.description: European leaders are pushing Prime Minister Viktor Orban to stop blocking a €90 billion loan to Kyiv. But the issue is a rallying cry in elections in Hungary set for April 12.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes, Jeanna Smialek and Cassandra Vinograd
item.media:description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban of Hungary in Budapest on Sunday. His disputes with President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine have become a focal point of Hungary’s coming election.

item.title: U.S. Lifts Fertilizer Sanctions on Belarus as Iran War Causes Price Surge
item.description: The Trump administration made the move in exchange for Belarus’s freeing of 250 political prisoners, part of a rapprochement between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Hopkins and Tomas Dapkus
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Sergey Ponomarev for The New York Times
item.media:description: Train cars carrying fertilizer ingredients from a Belarus factory in 2021. As global fertilizer prices soar, the U.S. has lifted sanctions on Belarus fertilizer products in exchange for the freeing of 250 political prisoners.

item.title: How Japan’s Leader Sanae Takaichi Found Her Voice in D.C. Decades Ago
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi got her start in politics as an intern for a trailblazing Colorado lawmaker. She returns to Washington this week to meet with President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández and Hisako Ueno

item.title: In Swaths of Germany, the Far-Right AfD Is Part of the Fabric
item.description: The Alternative for Germany party, or AfD, is shunned in federal politics but is a regional force. This fall, it could win broader power for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley, Tatiana Firsova and Clay Risen
item.media:credit: Sergey Ponomarev for The New York Times
item.media:description: Three AfD representatives — Christian Skotnik, left, Doreen Gärtner, center, and Enrico Schult — before a party meeting in Stavenhagen, Germany, in October.

item.title: 2 Men Charged With Spying for Iran on Jewish Institutions in UK
item.description: The men, both Iranian citizens, are accused of conducting surveillance on several Jewish institutions and community buildings in London.
item.dc:creator: Lizzie Dearden
item.media:credit: Andrew Testa for The New York Times
item.media:description: The entrance of the Bevis Marks Synagogue in London, which is more than 300 years old.

item.title: A Lebanese Cafe on the Edge of Destruction
item.description: Near the center of Beirut, one image captures the aftermath of an Israeli strike.
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: How TrumpRx Drug Prices Compare With Those in Other Countries
item.description: The TrumpRx website claims to offer the best prices for medications. Here’s where Americans still pay more — and much more.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Robbins
item.media:description: President Trump unveiled the TrumpRx website last month. Visitors to the site are greeted by a promise: “Find the world’s lowest prices on prescription drugs.”

item.title: Pakistan Pauses Afghanistan Airstrikes for Eid
item.description: At least 143 people were killed in a Pakistani airstrike that hit a drug rehabilitation facility in Kabul on Monday, according to a top U.N. official.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier, Safiullah Padshah and Zia ur-Rehman
item.media:description: A mass burial was held on Wednesday for people who died on Monday in a Pakistani airstrike on a drug rehabilitation center in Kabul, Afghanistan.

item.title: Retired Belgian Diplomat, 93, Faces Trial Over 1961 Killing of Congolese Leader
item.description: A Brussels court ruled that Étienne Davignon must stand trial for the murder of Patrice Lumumba, the first prime minister of the Democratic Republic of Congo.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.media:description: Patrice Lumumba, an independence leader who was the first Congolese prime minister, under detention in December 1960.

item.title: How Japan Reacted to Trump’s Pearl Harbor Joke
item.description: Some people criticized President Trump’s decision to invoke a painful chapter of history. Others worried it might harm U.S.-Japan relations.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández and Hisako Ueno
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump met with Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi of Japan in the Oval Office on Thursday.

item.title: Oil Falls and Stocks in Asia Hold Steady
item.description: Oil prices had been gyrating this week, after a new round of attacks on major energy facilities in Iran and Qatar raised concerns about energy supplies.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Using Charm and Restraint, Japan’s Leader Mostly Avoids Trump’s Wrath
item.description: During her first visit to the White House, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi drew praise from President Trump. But the war in the Middle East will test their relationship.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ms. Takaichi and President Trump in the Oval Office on Thursday.

item.title: Spain Says the Sun Shields It From Rising Gas Costs. Is That True?
item.description: Pedro Sánchez, Spain’s prime minister, said the nation’s renewable energy system has softened the financial fallout from the war in Iran. The story is more complex.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Sheep grazing on a pasture next to solar panels, following a major blackout on the Iberian Peninsula, in the village of Hostalric, near Girona, Spain, last year.

item.title: Iran War Fallout: Southeast Asia Hard Hit by Skyrocketing Fuel Prices
item.description: Across Southeast Asia, a region heavily dependent on energy exports brought via the Strait of Hormuz, lives are being upended by higher oil and gas prices.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee, Jason Gutierrez, Muktita Suhartono, Kittiphum Sringammuang and Tung Ngo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Lauren DeCicca for The New York Times
item.media:description: Fishermen on their boats while docked and unable to go out to sea due to diesel shortages and high prices, in Samut Sakhon, Thailand, on Monday.

item.title: How the Iran War Narrowed Flight Corridors Between Europe and Asia
item.description: One of the few paths left between the two continents threads through Azerbaijan, Georgia and Turkey, as global conflicts complicate aviation logistics.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Planes parked at Dubai International Airport on Wednesday, as airspace closures across the Middle East disrupted flight routes.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Thursday
item.description: Strikes continued in the region as attacks on energy infrastructure rattled global markets.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An oil refinery on Thursday in Haifa, Israel, that was damaged in an attack.

item.title: On Joe Rogan, Pierre Poilievre Talks Trump, Canada Tariffs and Bruce Lee
item.description: Floundering in the polls, Pierre Poilievre, the leader of Canada’s Conservatives, sat down with the popular podcaster in hopes of raising his standing at home and abroad.
item.dc:creator: Norimitsu Onishi
item.media:description: Pierre Poilievre, leader of Canada’s Conservative party, sat for a two-and-a-half-hour interview with Joe Rogan that was released on Thursday.

item.title: Is Russian Fuel Headed for Cuba, Testing the U.S. Blockade?
item.description: A Russian oil tanker is being closely tracked to see if it will challenge the Trump administration’s blockade on Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert and Jack Nicas
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Patriarch Ilia II, a Spiritual Symbol of Stability in Georgia, Dies at 93
item.description: The longest-serving leader in the history of the Georgian Orthodox Church, he helped guide his country in its transition from Soviet repression to modern statehood.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko
item.media:description: Thousands accompanied the patriarch’s coffin in a procession on Wednesday to the Holy Trinity Cathedral in Tbilisi.

item.title: Trump Says He Won’t Send Troops to Iran but Leaves Wiggle Room
item.description: The president was cagey about his plans for Iran. He confirmed the Pentagon was seeking $200 billion to support a protracted war effort while also claiming it would be over soon.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian and David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Thursday.

item.title: Trump Administration Faces Public Jitters as Oil Prices Surge Amid Iran War
item.description: President Trump and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel suggested that the war with Iran could end soon, but that there were still more attacks ahead.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm, David E. Sanger and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Trails and lights from missile defense interceptors are seen in the sky on Thursday, as excess gas is burned at an oil refinery in the northern Israel city of Haifa.

item.title: The Downfall of a Food Icon
item.description: We look at how Noma, the restaurant that revolutionized fine dining, became a byword for toxic kitchen culture.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Erik Refner for The New York Times

item.title: Trump’s Complaint About Israeli Strike on Gas Field Exposes Divergent Strategies
item.description: President Trump said he told Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel he disapproved of the attack, which sent energy markets reeling. But Israeli officials said the Americans were informed beforehand.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump has been trying to preserve Iran’s oil and gas infrastructure and keep the country from retaliating at energy facilities throughout the Persian Gulf.

item.title: ‘Africa Will Write Its Own History.’ Who Was Patrice Lumumba?
item.description: Remembering the African leader after a Brussels court this week ordered a retired Belgian diplomat stand trial for his 1961 assassination.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg
item.category: Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:description: Patrice Lumumba in New York in July 1960. He helped liberate Congo from brutal Belgian rule in 1960 and became the nation’s first prime minister after independence.

item.title: Jerusalem’s Old City sustains some damage after being largely spared in years of fighting.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.media:description: A roof was damaged by debris in East Jerusalem.

item.title: Trump Jokes About Pearl Harbor in Meeting With Japan’s Prime Minister
item.description: Breaking a taboo, President Trump needled Japan’s prime minister about the World War II attack, as she widened her eyes and appeared to take a deep breath in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández

item.title: U.S. Encourages Flow of Iranian Oil While It Battles Iran
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said removing sanctions on Iranian oil would lower global prices.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the sanctions exemption would apply to about 140 million barrels of Iranian oil that were currently at sea.

item.title: Body of Missing University of Alabama Student Is Found Dead in Spain, Police Say
item.description: James Gracey, 20, a student at the University of Alabama, disappeared during a visit to a beachfront nightclub in Barcelona. His body was found on Thursday, the police said.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe, Aimee Ortiz and Rylee Kirk

item.title: U.K. Advisers Sent to U.S. to Help Develop Options to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The move comes after President Trump sharply criticized Prime Minister Keir Starmer for not supporting his initial military strikes on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain said opening the Strait of Hormuz was “not a simple task.”

item.title: Israeli Officials Said U.S. Was Told About South Pars Attack
item.description: President Trump first said the United States “knew nothing” about an attack on the gas field in Iran, which sent global oil and gas prices soaring. He then said he cautioned Israel against it.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Refineries at the South Pars gas field in 2019.

item.title: European leaders seek a de-escalation of attacks.
item.dc:creator: Christopher F. Schuetze
item.media:description: President Emmanuel Macron of France with Chancellor Friedrich Merz of Germany in Brussels, on Thursday.

item.title: Father of a Palestinian woman killed with three others during a missile attack expresses shock.
item.description: The victims are the first Palestinians in the Israeli-occupied West Bank to be killed as a result of the Middle East war.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon
item.media:description: A funeral on Thursday for one of the women killed in the occupied West Bank.

item.title: Pentagon Seeks Additional $200 Billion to Fund Iran War
item.description: The request, which the White House has not yet submitted to Congress, is already encountering some resistance.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper, Tony Romm, Karoun Demirjian and Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth speaking to reporters on Thursday.

item.title: Why Iran’s Attack on an Energy Hub in Qatar Spooked Investors
item.description: Lasting damage to Ras Laffan Industrial City, the world’s largest liquefied natural gas export plant, would have big consequences for the global energy market.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Qatar blamed Iran for an attack that damaged the Ras Laffan terminal, the world’s largest liquefied natural gas facility.

item.title: Mapping two days of attacks on Gulf energy sites.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Leatherby

item.title: Saudi Official Warns Patience Is Limited as Iranian Attacks Barrage Kingdom
item.description: Prince Faisal bin Farhan, the foreign minister, said Saudi Arabia was prepared to take military action if necessary, after waves of missile and drone attacks.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: “We will not shy away from protecting our country and our economic resources,” the Saudi foreign minister, Prince Faisal bin Farhan, said on Thursday.

item.title: Useful Local Travel Apps to Download Before You Go Abroad
item.description: Ride-hailing, dining and navigation apps you rely on at home may not be the best options in many countries. Here are local alternatives to download before you go.
item.dc:creator: Ruffin Prevost

item.title: Trump Says U.S. and Qatar Not Involved in Strike on Iran’s South Pars Gas Field
item.description: President Trump said Israel was responsible for the attack, but vowed to “massively” destroy the gas field if Tehran hit Qatar’s energy facilities in retaliation.
item.dc:creator: Aurelien Breeden
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump said that Israel had “violently lashed out” at Iran’s South Pars gas field, hitting a “relatively small section” of the site.

item.title: The Weather Is Getting Wilder, and Some See a Dire Signal in the Data
item.description: Several of the Earth’s systems are changing faster than predicted as global temperatures rise, scientists say.
item.dc:creator: David Gelles
item.category: Potsdam Institute for Climate Impact Research
item.media:credit: Christinne Muschi/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A car crushed under a downed tree after strong winds in Montreal, Canada, on Tuesday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm, Isabel Kershner, David E. Sanger, Javier C. Hernández and Johnatan Reiss

item.title: Oil Gives Back Gains After Surging on Fears Over Energy Supplies
item.description: Attacks on major energy facilities in Iran and Qatar injected new uncertainty into the outlook for oil and gas prices.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Ozempic Is About to Go Generic in India, China and Canada
item.description: In India, China and several other nations, Novo Nordisk is on the verge of losing patent protection for its blockbuster weight loss drug, opening the door for cheaper competing versions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Robbins, Meaghan Tobin, Eshe Nelson, Alex Travelli and Pragati K.B.
item.media:credit: Saumya Khandelwal for The New York Times

item.title: Iranian Missile Attack Leaves 3 Palestinians Dead in West Bank
item.description: Several others were injured in the strike, which hit a caravan being used as a hair salon in the town of Beit Awwa, according to Palestinian officials.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Fatima AbdulKarim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Palestinian security forces gathering at the scene where three Palestinian women were killed in an Iranian missile attack near Hebron in the Israeli-occupied West Bank on Wednesday.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Wednesday
item.description: A day of strikes on energy facilities and testimony in Washington to members of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ras Laffan Industrial City, Qatar, earlier this month. A state-owned Qatari energy company said that Ras Laffan Industrial City had sustained “extensive damage” from missile attacks.

item.title: Iran Turns Out for Funeral for Those Killed in Strikes
item.description: Some died in Iran, others on a sea far from home. They were honored together Wednesday at a procession in Tehran.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Days of Anger and Fear for Iranians Living With Waves of Airstrikes
item.description: Residents in Tehran and elsewhere say the attacks come at all hours and rock the ground.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Apartments in a neighborhood of Tehran targeted by American-Israeli airstrikes this week.

item.title: 2 Charged With Surveillance for Iran on London’s Jews
item.description: The suspects are accused of gathering information last summer on targets linked to the city’s Jewish community.
item.dc:creator: Lizzie Dearden
item.category: Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)
item.media:description: Metropolitan Police headquarters in London. This week’s prosecutions are the latest of several related to hostile activity in Britain backed by Iran.

item.title: Empowering Iran’s Hard-liners
item.description: U.S.-Israeli attacks helped ultraconservatives cement power. That could make ending the war tougher.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Palestinian Man Recounts Brutal Sexual Assault by Israeli Settlers
item.description: The man said his attackers stripped him naked, beat him and zip-tied his genitalia, an account corroborated by family members and a rights activist who were also beaten.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger and Fatima AbdulKarim
item.media:credit: Afif Amireh for The New York Times
item.media:description: Suhaib Abualkebash, with a bruised eye, in Khirbet Humsa, in the Israeli-occupied West Bank. He said he had been assaulted by Israeli settlers.

item.title: Strikes Deliver Another Blow to Iran’s Domestic Energy Sites
item.description: The attacks could worsen the severe electricity shortages that Iranians already face.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir, Christina Goldbaum and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Part of the South Pars gas field facility near Kangan, Iran, in 2014. The extent of the damage on Wednesday was not immediately clear.

item.title: Iran Maintains Near-Total Internet Blackout Amid U.S.-Israeli Strikes
item.description: As the war has stretched into its third week, the Iranian government has blocked internet access for most of its 92 million citizens.
item.dc:creator: Parin Behrooz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A woman with her phone at a market in Tehran this month. An internet blackout has cut off the majority of Iranians from communicating with the outside world.

item.title: Why Trump Keeps Saying ‘Nuclear’
item.description: Our national security reporter David E. Sanger describes how President Trump is considering a risky commando operation at an Iranian nuclear site.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Coleman Lowndes, Joey Sendaydiego, Estelle Caswell, Jon Hazell, Rafaela Balster and Whitney Shefte

item.title: The Tropical St. Patrick’s Day That Honors African History
item.description: Montserrat treats the holiday as both a national celebration and a more somber milestone: a commemoration of a failed slave rebellion.
item.dc:creator: James Wagner and Luis Antonio Rojas

item.title: U.S. Intelligence Saw No Change in Iran’s Missile Capabilities Before War
item.description: On Wednesday, the director of national intelligence and C.I.A. director contradicted one of the justifications the Trump administration had given for its attacks on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Tulsi Gabbard, the director of national intelligence, testified before the Senate Intelligence Committee on Wednesday.
